title: Plane Matching Decomposition Problem
statement_text: Does there exist a constant $c>0$ such that every complete geometric graph on $n$ vertices can be partitioned into at most $cn$ plane matchings?
context_text: In 2005, Araujo, Dumitrescu, Hurtado, Noy, and Urrutia asked whether the edge set of every complete geometric graph can be partitioned into a small number of crossing-free matchings. For convex position the answer is $n-1$ (or $n$), but the best known upper bound for general position is only $O(n^{3/2})$, which seems far off.
attributed_to: Araujo, Dumitrescu, Hurtado, Noy, and Urrutia
paper_contribution: Proves that for $n$ random points in $[0,1]^2$, with probability tending to 1, the edge set of $K_n[A]$ can be covered by $O(n\log n)$ crossing-free paths and $O(n\sqrt{\log n})$ crossing-free matchings, improving the general $O(n^{3/2})$ bound from [3] for this setting.
notes: The problem header uses the LaTeX auto-counter macro \thetheorem rather than an explicit number; attribution is given unambiguously in the surrounding prose referencing [3].
paper_title: Covering Complete Geometric Graphs by Monotone Paths

title: Conjecture 1.4
statement_text: For some (possibly, for every) dense $n$-element point set $A$ in general position in the plane, covering the edge set of $K_{n}[A]$ requires a superlinear number of monotone paths.
context_text: Theorem 1.3 shows that $O(n^{3/2})$ monotone paths suffice to cover $K_n[A]$ for any $\alpha$-dense point set $A$. The authors suspect this upper bound is not tight and that a superlinear lower bound holds. This is contrasted with the worst-case arbitrary point sets, for which a quadratic number of monotone paths is required.
paper_title: Covering Complete Geometric Graphs by Monotone Paths

title: Problem 5.1
statement_text: Does there exist a constant $c>1/2$ with the property that, for infinitely many values of $n$, there are $n$-element point sets $A$ in the plane such that every covering of the edge set of $K_{n}[A]$ by crossing-free paths requires at least $cn$ paths?
context_text: Pach, Saghafian, and Schnider proved that every complete convex geometric graph can be decomposed into $n/2 + o(n)$ plane subgraphs. It is open whether a linear lower bound strictly above $n/2$ holds for crossing-free path coverings over general point sets.
paper_title: Covering Complete Geometric Graphs by Monotone Paths

title: Problem 5.2
statement_text: What is the smallest number $z=z(n)$ such that the edge set of every complete geometric graph on $n$ vertices can be covered by $z$ plane zig-zag paths?
context_text: The authors show that for any finite point set $A$ in general position in the plane, one can cover the edge set of $K_n[A]$ by plane Hamiltonian zig-zag paths, but the minimum number of such paths needed in the worst case is left open.
paper_title: Covering Complete Geometric Graphs by Monotone Paths
